title: angelica muliensis
intro: Angelica muliensis, also known as dong quai, is a type of flowering plant in the carrot family, native to China and other parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, known for its medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-aging effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing menstrual cramps, improving circulation, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of dong quai can be found in teas, capsules, and tinctures, with some also using the plant's roots and leaves in cooking and as a culinary ingredient.
